goal: Move toward healthier, more sustainable choices; reduce takeout while fitting the family schedule.
Lower processed foods and added sugar; keep grocery spending to roughly $100-130 per week.
Reduce meat consumption and food waste while keeping meal prep simple.
constraints: Time commitment: 45-60 minutes on Sunday for meal prep and around 30 minutes on weeknights.
Weekly grocery budget target: $100–130 per week.
Evening kitchen closed by 8:30 pm with a small allowed bedtime snack if needed (for adults/kids).
Keep changes low-effort; avoid increasing overall weekly prep time beyond current targets (Sunday 45–60 minutes; weeknights ~30 minutes).
decisions: Following a 3-week trial plan focusing on takeout reduction and snack management.
Trial plan to reduce processed foods and added sugars within a $100–130/week budget.
Adopt structured snacking as main strategy to reduce grazing; implement a gentle evening kitchen closed rule.
Adopt a two-week rotating template built from staple ingredients.

Proteins to rotate (with rationale and usage) including chicken, ground beef, pork, salmon, white fish, canned fish, eggs, legumes, tofu & dairy; weekly guidelines: Poultry 2–3 dinners/week; Red meat 1–2; Fish 1–2; Eggs & dairy 2–4; Plant proteins 2–3.
Adopt kid-friendly, low-salt, low-sugar sauce/dressing swaps; batch prep and store.
Introduce a 1-week kid helper schedule for simple tasks to support meals.
Adopt a compact two-week shopping list method to speed store runs.
Create a three-step process to derive and group list by store sections from templates.
Use a printable or mobile-friendly version of the two-week shopping list for quick reference.
Create a go-to reheatable meals list for activity nights with storage and reheating tips.
Adopt a two-meal-per-week plant-forward plan with batch-cooked legumes on Sunday; implement a weekly leftovers night and a simple repurposing strategy.
Adopt frozen vegetables as budget-stabilizing fallback within flexible meal planning.
Implement a quick action plan to stock frozen staples and maintain a 1–2 week pantry buffer.
Track unit prices and plan purchases around sales to smooth the grocery budget.
